insight: Shift SEO goals from rankings and traffic to AI visibility by tracking citations, mentions, and inclusion in generative responses.
quote: If you aren’t cited in that response, you don’t exist.

insight: Optimize content for semantic relevance using vector embeddings and cosine similarity to ensure alignment with queries and subqueries.
quote: Compare those embeddings using cosine similarity to get a score.

insight: Structure content at the passage level so each section independently answers a specific query or subquery for AI retrieval.

source.name: Michael King - How AI Mode Works

quote: LLMs retrieve and reason at the passage level not the whole page.

insight: Increase entity richness (linked, verifiable entities like people, brands, concepts) to improve AI understanding and citation likelihood.
quote: Provides 'Knowledge Graph anchors' for AI systems.

insight: Ensure content delivers unique insights (information gain) instead of repeating existing SERP content to improve AI selection.
Write concise, direct, and answer-first content; avoid fluff and overly complex explanations that reduce AI extractability.
quote: AI engines often look for the most direct, specific answer.

insight: Optimize for query fan-out by covering multiple related subtopics and intents, not just a single keyword.

source.name: Michael King - How AI Mode Works

quote: You’re optimizing for your content to be semantically relevant across dozens of hidden queries.

insight: Create modular, well-structured content (clear sections, comparisons, facts) so AI systems can easily extract and recombine information.

source.name: Michael King - How AI Mode Works

quote: Content needs to be modular and extractable.

insight: Include comparative and decision-oriented content (pros/cons, alternatives, recommendations) to align with AI reasoning and ranking.

source.name: Michael King - How AI Mode Works

quote: Content that articulates pros, cons, and 'why X over Y' survives better.

insight: Balance depth with clarity—avoid overly long or complex content unless it increases factual density and entity coverage.
quote: Increasing word count without increasing entity count decreased citation probability.

insight: Maintain strong traditional rankings (top 5–10) as a baseline, since AI systems still rely on trusted indexed sources.
quote: To be cited by an AI, you first have to be trusted by the index.

insight: Expand content strategy to multimodal formats (video, audio, visuals) to increase inclusion across AI-generated outputs.

source.name: Michael King - How AI Mode Works

quote: Relying solely on text-based content risks being left out altogether.

insight: Run controlled experiments on content changes (entities, structure, depth) instead of applying broad SEO best practices.
quote: Run controlled experiments... not to over-optimize the entire site at once.
